summary: A Go-based test orchestration framework using numbered agents to incrementally build and validate a testing infrastructure with TUI reporting and MCP handler support
patterns: agent-based orchestration with numbered agent directories
test-driven development with extensive extra test coverage
command pattern via cmd/ directory structure
TUI (Terminal User Interface) with bubbletea framework
concerns: large number of agent directories (40+) may indicate fragmented development or experimentation
truncated directory names (e.g., internalschema-validatio) suggest filesystem limitations
heavy test file presence could indicate test code mixed with orchestration metadata
potential coupling between agent orchestration layer and core application logic
suggestions: consolidate or archive completed agent directories to reduce clutter
document the agent numbering scheme and purpose of each agent wave
ensure clear separation between orchestration metadata and application source code
consider extracting the agent framework into a reusable tool
add integration tests that validate agent outputs holistically
